title: Implement FailureExplainerService (service layer)
context: Part of the Failure Explainer API for Mission Control. See full design at docs/failure-explainer-design.md. This is task 1 of 3 — the core service logic. No LLM involved; pure deterministic rule evaluation over DB records.

spec.main_method: async def explain(self, task_id: str) -> dict | None
spec.pydantic_models_in: app/schemas.py or inline in service file

rules.condition: Any WorkerRun for task has ended_at IS NOT NULL AND succeeded IS NULL
rules.explanation_template: A worker handling this task appears to have crashed. Run {run_id} ended at {ended_at} without recording a success/failure outcome.
next_actions: Check OpenClaw worker logs for the crash time
Force-fail the task and retry
If recurring, check system memory/process limits

rules.condition: Any WorkerRun has timeout_reason IS NOT NULL
rules.explanation_template: Worker run timed out: {timeout_reason}. The task exceeded its runtime budget.
next_actions: Consider breaking the task into smaller subtasks
Review task scope — it may be too large for one agent run
If appropriate, assign a higher model tier with longer context

rules.condition: A ModelUsageEvent with error_code LIKE '%budget%' OR error_code LIKE '%quota%' exists within the time window of a failed WorkerRun for this task (join on started_at/ended_at range, or correlate by session if available)
rules.explanation_template: A model budget cap was hit during task execution. Budget lane: {budget_lane}.
next_actions: Check current lane caps at GET /api/usage/budget-lanes
Consider raising the daily cap for the affected lane (PATCH /api/usage/budget-lanes)
Or wait for cap reset at midnight UTC

rules.explanation_template: This task has failed {failed_run_count} times. Most recent failure: {last_failure_reason}.
next_actions: Review the pattern of failures — are they the same root cause?
Consider assigning a stronger model tier (strong)
Break the task into smaller, more targeted subtasks
If the issue is unclear, escalate to human review

rules.condition: Task.escalation_tier >= 3 AND Task.work_state NOT IN ('completed') AND Task.updated_at < now() - 4 hours
rules.explanation_template: Task has reached escalation tier {escalation_tier} and has not progressed in {stuck_duration_minutes} minutes.
next_actions: Manually review the task and decide on a path forward
If diagnostic agent was spawned, check its outcome
Consider rejecting and re-scoping the task

rules.explanation_template: Task has been in-progress for {stuck_duration_minutes} minutes with no state change.
next_actions: Check if a worker session is still running for this task
If no active session, force-fail and retry via orchestrator
Check system health at /api/health

rules.condition: Latest WorkerRun.succeeded == True AND (WorkerRun.files_modified is empty or null) AND (WorkerRun.commit_shas is empty or null)
rules.explanation_template: The last run reported success but produced no file changes or commits. Work may not have been saved.
next_actions: Check the worker's session transcript for what was actually done
Verify the git working directory on the host
Consider re-running the task with explicit artifact requirements in the prompt

rules.condition: Latest WorkerRun.succeeded == True AND WorkerRun.summary IS NULL OR empty — but files_modified IS non-empty (worker did something but didn't write .work-summary)
rules.explanation_template: The last run completed but did not produce a work summary. Hard to verify what was done.
next_actions: Review the git diff for the commit to understand what was changed
Add .work-summary output requirement to the task template for this agent type

rules.condition: Task.status == 'active' AND Task.work_state == 'not_started' AND Task.created_at < now() - 2 hours AND COUNT(WorkerRun for task) == 0
rules.explanation_template: Task has been active for {stuck_duration_minutes} minutes but no worker has picked it up.
next_actions: Check orchestrator scanner is running (/api/orchestrator/status)
Verify the task has an assigned agent or is eligible for auto-routing
Check if agent capacity is exhausted

rules.explanation_template: Task was rejected/cancelled without a recorded reason.
next_actions: Add a failure_reason to this task for audit trail
Review who rejected this task and why
See /api/inbox/stuck-remediations if this was from an inbox approval flow

notes: Rules are evaluated in priority order (1=highest). First matching rule = primary_failure_code. ALL matching rules are returned in all_failure_codes.
If no rules match, return primary_failure_code=None, all_failure_codes=[], severity=None.
Use Task.created_at for SLA_miss_first_response if started_at is null.
ModelUsageEvent correlation for budget_lane_exhausted: query events where created_at is within (run.started_at, run.ended_at). If run.ended_at is null, use run.started_at to run.started_at+2h.
cancel_reason may not exist as a Task column — check models.py. If absent, only check failure_reason for AC-10.

acceptance: FailureExplainerService.explain() correctly classifies all 10 failure mode fixtures defined in AC-01 through AC-10 of the design doc. AC-11 (negative case) returns null primary_failure_code. All rules are unit-testable without HTTP layer.

title: Implement /api/intelligence router and register endpoint
context: Part of the Failure Explainer API. Depends on Task 1 (FailureExplainerService). Creates the new intelligence router and registers it in main.py. See docs/failure-explainer-design.md.

endpoints.description: Returns a structured failure explanation for a specific task
endpoints.returns_200_with_null_codes_when: task found but no failure signals detected

spec.main_py_registration: Include router with prefix='/api' in app/main.py — follow the same pattern as reliability.py router registration

acceptance: GET /api/intelligence/tasks/{valid_id}/failure-explanation returns 200 with correct schema. GET /api/intelligence/tasks/nonexistent returns 404. Endpoint requires valid Bearer token (401 without it).

title: Write acceptance tests for all 10 failure modes
context: Part of the Failure Explainer API. Depends on Tasks 1 and 2. Writes the full test suite per the acceptance cases in docs/failure-explainer-design.md (Section 6). Tests are pure unit tests of FailureExplainerService — no HTTP required except one integration smoke test.

spec.db_setup: Use async in-memory SQLite (same pattern as other tests in tests/ directory)
each_test_must: Create minimal fixture Task (and WorkerRun/ModelUsageEvent as needed)
notes: Use datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(hours=5) for 'old' timestamps to trigger stuck/escalation rules
Do NOT mock datetime — instead inject a 'now' parameter to the service or use DB timestamps that are clearly in the past

For AC-03 (budget_lane_exhausted): if ModelUsageEvent time-window correlation is too complex to test without a worker run, it's acceptable to test via a separate helper method that classifies by error_code alone

acceptance: pytest tests/test_failure_explainer.py passes all 11 test cases. No flaky time-based assertions (use explicit old timestamps). All tests run in <5 seconds total.
